stories.headline: At least 10 killed in massive overnight Russian attack on Kyiv, officials say
stories.summary: The attack, which was still underway early Monday morning, involved waves of ballistic and cruise missiles as well as drones. Explosions echoed across the city as civilians sought shelter in metro stations.

stories.headline: Russia launches deadly missile and drone attacks on Kyiv

stories.headline: Europe Today: Ukraine, NATO and the future of EU enlargement
stories.summary: Russia launches another deadly attack on Kyiv ahead of the NATO summit as Ukraine seeks stronger military support and progress on EU membership. Meanwhile, thousands protest against Germany's far-right AfD as the European Commission prepares reforms to the EU enlargement process.

stories.headline: Moody’s Warns Asia-Pacific Private Credit Expansion to Slow
stories.summary: Growth in private credit fundraising and deployment in the Asia Pacific will slow over the next 12 to 18 months as macroeconomic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions, and elevated interest rates weigh on investor appetite for illiquid assets, according to Moody’s Ratings.

stories.headline: Iron Ore Rises on Fortescue Curbs and as Slump Spurs Buying
stories.summary: Iron ore rose after China’s state-backed buyer of the commodity expanded curbs on Australian miner Fortescue Ltd., and as a slump in prices over the last couple of months attracted buyers.

stories.headline: Sara Duterte Presidential Dream at Stake in Trial
stories.summary: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a contentious trial in the Senate from Monday, a case that could end her political career or leave her as the top contender to succeed archrival Ferdinand Marcos Jr. as president. Bloomberg's Manila Bureau CHief Manolo Serapio breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran war live: Khamenei’s funeral procession under way in Tehran
stories.summary: The funeral procession is expected to last 10-12 hours and has drawn crowds of hundreds of thousands of mourners.

stories.headline: India's Modi to Tour Indo-Pacific This Week
stories.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is due to travel to Indonesia, Australia and New Zealand this week. Bloomberg's Swati Pandey discusses the agenda for the leader's Indo-Pacific tour. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: NSE Said to Start Marketing $3 Billion India IPO Next Week
stories.summary: National Stock Exchange of India Ltd., the operator of the world’s largest derivatives exchange by trading volume, is set to begin formal marketing as early as next week, according to people familiar with the matter, moving ahead with what could be one of the largest-ever initial public offerings in the country.

stories.headline: China’s Biggest ETF Is Now a Gold Fund as National Team Retreats
stories.summary: A China spot gold ETF has overtaken the Huatai-PineBridge CSI 300 ETF to become the country’s largest exchange-traded fund, highlighting the extent to which state-backed support for the stock market has faded.

stories.headline: Unease Over Korea Leveraged ETFs Grows as Lawmakers Demand Fix
stories.summary: South Korean lawmakers are increasingly sounding alarms over the risks of single‑stock leveraged exchange‑traded funds, with an opposition party member now calling for such products to be delisted.

stories.headline: Copper and Aluminum Advance on Fading Expectations for Rate Hike
stories.summary: Copper rose for a third day and aluminum extended a rebound from a four-month low, supported by fading expectations that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates.

stories.headline: CarbonScape COO on CATL Investment & Partnership
stories.summary: Vincent Ledoux-Pedailles, Chief Commercial Officer at CarbonScape, discusses the deal that will see Chinese battery giant CATL and Hong Kong–based Lochpine Capital take a 20% strategic stake in the company. CarbonScape converts forestry byproducts into graphite for lithium batteries. stories.headline: Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella pledge unity ahead of judicial verdict

stories.summary: A rebound for Asian technology stocks stalled as investors turned cautious about the durability of the AI-driven rally. The dollar rose against most of its major peers.

stories.headline: 8 Wounded in Coney Island Shooting on Fourth of July

stories.headline: America Marks Its 250th Birthday With Storms, Sweat and Fire
